what_it_does: Documents an auxiliary cross-encoder reranker model (cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2) that is present on disk but not yet served, and frames it as a future candidate for an explicit Reranking need-category in the models hub.
observations: Disk size is 88 MB and architecture is a 6-layer MiniLM cross-encoder trained on MS-MARCO
Status is auxiliary and runtime is not-yet-served
Date added is recorded as present-before-substrate-setup
Tags include foundation, models, reranker, auxiliary
Describes the two-stage retrieval pattern: dense retrieve top-N then cross-encoder rerank top-K
Lists open decisions: whether to elevate reranking to a named need-category, performance characteristics, and whether a bigger reranker would be preferable
Connects back to the models index hub
standouts: A pre-existing HF-cache model being catalogued before deliberate substrate setup
Explicitly flagged as not-yet-served despite being present on disk
Proposes elevating reranking from an implicit pattern to a named hub need-category
concerns: Runtime says not-yet-served while the model is present on disk, leaving the activation path undefined
No performance, latency, or benchmark numbers are provided
Open comparison to a larger reranker is unresolved
notes: This is a ledger stub for a small MS-MARCO cross-encoder reranker that the project may later promote into a named Reranking need-category once retrieval quality work begins.
questions: What does present-before-substrate-setup mean operationally for ownership and serving?
When or under what conditions will reranking be elevated to a named need-category?
What are the latency/throughput and ranking-quality characteristics of this specific model in the target runtime?
How will the model be served if it moves from not-yet-served to active?
Is this model intended as the default reranker, or only as a baseline to compare against larger rerankers?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to record an auxiliary reranker model and its possible role; the file does exactly that, including metadata, rationale, and open questions.
apparent_themes: Two-stage retrieval with dense retrieval plus cross-encoder reranking
intention_signals: The file is trying to surface a latent, already-available reranker asset and prepare the ground for making reranking a first-class need-category in the models hub when retrieval work becomes a priority.
novelty: It treats reranking as a real but not-yet-named production pattern and records a pre-substrate model as a candidate for future elevation, rather than as an active component.
contribution: Records the identity, size, architecture, source, and intended retrieval role of the MS-MARCO MiniLM reranker; preserves the open question of whether reranking should become an explicit hub need-category; and documents the two-stage retrieval rationale for later fusion decisions.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger entry for the auxiliary cross-encoder reranker cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2, an 88 MB MS-MARCO-trained MiniLM model present on disk but not yet served, positioned as a future candidate for a Reranking need-category in the models hub.
intention_for_embedding: To catalogue a pre-existing small reranker and keep it ready for promotion into an explicit reranking need-category when retrieval quality work arises.
